formulas.name: The Government of India Act, 1935
formulas.formula: A British law that served as a framework for the government of India.
formulas.notes: This act laid the foundation for the Constitution of India.

formulas.name: The Cabinet Mission Plan, 1946
formulas.formula: A plan proposed by the British Government to grant independence to India.
formulas.notes: This plan proposed a federal structure for India with a weak centre and strong provinces.

formulas.name: The Objectives Resolution, 1946
formulas.formula: A resolution moved by Jawaharlal Nehru in the Constituent Assembly.
formulas.notes: This resolution outlined the basic principles of the Constitution of India.

formulas.name: The Constituent Assembly Debates, 1946-1949
formulas.formula: A series of debates held by the Constituent Assembly to draft the Constitution.
formulas.notes: These debates were crucial in shaping the final draft of the Constitution.

formulas.name: The Directive Principles of State Policy, 1947
formulas.formula: A set of guidelines for the government to follow while making laws.
formulas.notes: These principles were enshrined in Part IV of the Constitution.

formulas.name: The Preamble to the Constitution of India, 1949
formulas.formula: A brief introduction to the Constitution.
formulas.notes: This preamble sets out the objectives and ideals of the Constitution.

formulas.name: The Fundamental Rights, 1949
formulas.formula: A set of rights guaranteed to all citizens of India.
formulas.notes: These rights are enshrined in Part III of the Constitution.

formulas.name: The Supreme Court, 1950
formulas.formula: The highest court in India, responsible for interpreting the Constitution.
formulas.notes: This court was established under Article 124 of the Constitution.
